name: Amazon Bedrock
description: AWS multi-vendor model-hosting platform with IAM, provisioned throughput, and broad compliance

provider.comment: Operated by AWS as a fully managed, serverless model-hosting platform.

category.comment: Hyperscaler platform serving 100+ foundation models from many vendors (Amazon, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Moonshot, MiniMax, OpenAI, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, AI21) behind one API with IAM, regions, and compliance.

api-launched.comment: Amazon Bedrock reached general availability on 2023-09-28 (announced in preview 2023-04-13). Exact GA date corroborated via AWS What's New announcement.

status.comment: Developer API is generally available with published commercial pricing; individual newer models may be in preview.

api-docs.comment: Official Amazon Bedrock User Guide and API reference.

auth-methods.comment: The bedrock-runtime endpoint uses AWS SigV4 request signing backed by IAM identity-based policies; a newer bedrock-mantle endpoint additionally supports Amazon Bedrock API keys and the OpenAI SDK (checked 2026-07).

self-serve-signup.comment: Fully self-serve with immediate pay-as-you-go activation via the AWS console — no sales contact required. Note it does require creating/having an AWS account (there is no standalone Bedrock-only key issued outside AWS).

free-credits-trial.comment: No standing free tier for Bedrock model inference could be confirmed from the pricing page; new AWS accounts may receive general promotional AWS credits, but no dedicated Bedrock free-inference tier is documented (checked 2026-07).

regional-endpoints.comment: Available across many AWS regions in the US, Europe, and Asia Pacific (plus AWS GovCloud); cross-region inference profiles keep traffic within a chosen geography.

data-residency-options.comment: Customers pin processing to a chosen AWS region; cross-region inference is scoped to a geography (e.g. US-only or EU-only profiles), a contractual/config data-residency guarantee.

model-breadth.comment: Serves model families from many vendors: Amazon (Nova/Titan), Anthropic, DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, MiniMax, OpenAI, Meta, Mistral, Cohere, AI21, Stability AI.

open-weight-models.comment: Open-weight models available including Meta Llama, Mistral, DeepSeek, and OpenAI gpt-oss.

model-version-pinning.comment: Models are invoked by immutable dated model IDs (e.g. anthropic.claude-opus-4-7), so a specific snapshot can be pinned.

custom-fine-tuned-deployment.comment: Supports fine-tuning (e.g. Llama, Cohere), model distillation, and Custom Model Import to deploy and call your own/private models through the same API.

model-catalog-size.comment: Documentation states 100+ foundation models from industry-leading providers (checked 2026-07).

chat-messages-endpoint.comment: Modern multi-turn messages endpoints: the native Converse API plus OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions and Responses APIs and native InvokeModel.

streaming.comment: Token streaming via ConverseStream and InvokeModelWithResponseStream (SSE-style incremental responses).

tool-function-calling.comment: Documented tool use / function calling via the Converse API toolConfig (and equivalent OpenAI-compatible function calling), model-dependent.

parallel-tool-calls.comment: Capable models (e.g. Claude, Nova) can emit multiple tool-use blocks in a single Converse turn for concurrent execution; availability depends on the underlying model.

structured-output.comment: Server-enforced Structured Outputs validate responses against a JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12 subset) via Converse outputConfig.textFormat or the OpenAI-compatible response_format (json_schema); looser JSON mode (json_object) also supported. Support varies by model (checked 2026-07).

system-prompt.comment: Converse API exposes a distinct system field; OpenAI-compatible endpoints use the system/developer role.

image-input-vision.comment: Vision-capable models (Claude, Amazon Nova, Llama vision) accept image input via the Converse API image content block.

audio-input.comment: Amazon Nova Sonic speech models accept audio input through a dedicated Bedrock bidirectional streaming API (speech understanding).

document-pdf-input.comment: Converse API supports a document content block (PDF, DOCX, etc.) as first-class input rather than pasted text; model-dependent.

image-generation.comment: Image-generation models available on the same API/account: Amazon Nova Canvas, Titan Image Generator, and Stability AI models.

text-to-speech-audio-output.comment: Amazon Nova Sonic is a speech-to-speech model producing audio output via a Bedrock streaming API on the same account.

embeddings-endpoint.comment: Text/multimodal embeddings available on the same API: Amazon Titan Text Embeddings, Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings, and Cohere Embed.

prompt-caching.comment: Explicit prompt caching supported (caller marks cache points); cached input read is billed at a steep discount (e.g. ~10% of standard input rate for Claude models).

batch-api.comment: Asynchronous batch inference available at 50% lower price than on-demand for select models.

max-context-window.comment: 1,000,000-token context supported by Amazon Nova Premier / Nova 2 and by Anthropic Claude (1M-token beta) on Bedrock. Largest window varies by model and some advertise more; 1M is the reliably confirmed large window (checked 2026-07).

extended-reasoning.comment: Reasoning/thinking exposed at the API level: Claude extended thinking, Amazon Nova 2 adjustable 'thinking', and OpenAI reasoning models via the Responses API.

built-in-web-search.comment: No first-party web-search/grounding tool exposed via the inference API. AgentCore offers a managed Browser tool for web automation and Knowledge Bases for RAG, but not a hosted web-search tool comparable to OpenAI/Gemini grounding (checked 2026-07).

built-in-code-execution.comment: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Code Interpreter provides a first-party sandboxed code-execution tool (multi-language runtimes, internet access, CloudTrail logging).

mcp-support.comment: Bedrock AgentCore Gateway acts as a managed Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, exposing APIs/Lambda functions as MCP tools with native MCP support and outbound auth (checked 2026-07).

files-retrieval-api.comment: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases provide managed retrieval/RAG over uploaded/ingested documents the model can reference.

agents-assistants-api.comment: Higher-level stateful agents via Amazon Bedrock Agents and Bedrock AgentCore (deploy/operate agents with any framework and model, managed memory and tool orchestration).

openai-compatible-api.comment: The bedrock-mantle endpoint exposes OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions and Responses APIs usable directly with the OpenAI SDK via a base-URL swap (AWS-operated, separate from OpenAI's hosted API); checked 2026-07.

official-sdks.comment: Accessed through the first-party AWS SDKs, which cover Python (boto3), JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, .NET, Rust, and PHP; the OpenAI and Anthropic SDKs also work against the compatible endpoints.

native-api-style.comment: Native primary style is the AWS cloud-native Converse / InvokeModel API (bedrock-runtime); OpenAI Chat Completions/Responses and Anthropic Messages are additional compatibility shims.

webhooks-callbacks.comment: Async batch job completion is signalled through AWS EventBridge events and S3 output notifications rather than classic HTTP webhooks (checked 2026-07).

published-sla.comment: AWS publishes an Amazon Bedrock Service Level Agreement (monthly uptime commitment with service-credit remedies). Figure not re-verified against the specific SLA document this pass (checked 2026-07).

rate-limit-model.comment: Default per-model, per-region request/token quotas (adjustable via AWS Service Quotas) plus reserved capacity via Provisioned Throughput (1-month / 6-month commitments).

provider-fallback-routing.comment: Amazon Bedrock Intelligent Prompt Routing can route requests across models by prompt to balance cost/quality; cross-region inference profiles route for capacity. Not a full cross-provider failover gateway (checked 2026-07).

status-page.comment: Covered by the public AWS Health Dashboard (status.aws.amazon.com); CloudWatch/CloudTrail provide per-account monitoring.

no-training-default.comment: AWS states customer data is not shared with model providers and is not used to train the base models; fine-tuning operates on a private model copy.

zero-data-retention.comment: Bedrock does not store or log prompts and completions by default for inference; data is encrypted in transit and at rest, with PrivateLink/VPC isolation available (checked 2026-07).

compliance-certifications.comment: SOC compliant, ISO 27001 certified, HIPAA eligible, GDPR compliant, and FedRAMP High authorized (AWS GovCloud); CSA STAR Level 2 also noted. PCI DSS not confirmed on the security page this pass.

enterprise-sso-access-control.comment: AWS IAM identity-based policies plus AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO/SAML) provide org-level access control and federation.

pricing-model.comment: Primarily per-token on-demand usage pricing; reserved capacity via Provisioned Throughput (hourly, 1-/6-month commitments). Batch inference at 50% off.

unified-billing.comment: A single AWS account/bill covers all models and vendors reachable through Bedrock.

cache-batch-discount.comment: Batch inference ~50% off on-demand; prompt-cache reads discounted (e.g. ~10% of standard input rate for Claude).

cheapest-entry.comment: Lowest-friction paid start is self-serve pay-as-you-go on-demand per-token pricing via the AWS console.
